text: The distal rectal cuff is visible through the anal port following a transanal total mesorectal excision.
The surgeon places a purse string suture on the distal rectal cuff through the anal port, preparing for the next phase of the procedure.
The surgeon places a purse string suture on the distal rectal cuff using standard surgical instruments, visible through the anal port, as part of the transanal total mesorectal excision procedure.
The surgeon places a purse string suture around the circumference of the distal rectal cuff, leaving the suture ends untied and free.
The surgeon inserts a standard ten French ReadyVac drain through the center of the purse string suture on the rectal cuff, advancing the drain into the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon knots the purse string suture tightly around the ten French ReadyVac drain, which is now visibly positioned within the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon feeds the spindle of a standard 28mm circular stapler into the distal end of the drain, preparing for the next step in the procedure.
The surgeon advances the drain through the purse string into the abdominal cavity, guiding the spindle of the attached circular stapler through the center of the purse string.
The laparoscopic operator visualizes the spindle of the circular stapler fully within the abdominal cavity before carefully removing the ReadyVac drain from the spindle.
The surgeon forms the stapled anastomosis by closing the circular stapler, ensuring the anal port is removed prior to closure.
